title: Wesley Vandesteene is 2026's real underdog rise
meta: Published: 17 March 2026 | F1CC News Feature Desk
caption: Lead image: Wesley Vandesteene in the Mercedes garage.
body:
If you want a true underdog arc in 2026, start with Wesley Vandesteene. His 2025 profile was modest: 21st in the standings with 32 points. Useful pace, limited return, and little reason to expect he would be one of the names shaping the top of the table this early in the next season.

Three rounds into 2026, that picture has changed quickly. Vandesteene's second place in Australia was not a lucky cameo from nowhere; it was a composed race under extreme conditions that delivered Mercedes one of the most valuable single-race results of the early year. Even with a difficult China weekend, he sits on 18 points and remains in the front section of the standings.

The key shift is not just points. It is race quality. In 2025, Vandesteene looked like a driver fighting for survival in a crowded midfield. In 2026, he has looked like a driver who can convert key moments when the front-running teams hesitate. That is a very different skill set, and it is exactly what turns a fringe name into a season-long factor.

He is not the title favourite, and no one is pretending otherwise. But that is exactly why this is the underdog story worth tracking. From 32 points and 21st last year to a podium and early relevance this year, Vandesteene has already done enough to change how the paddock talks about him. The next step is proving this is the new baseline, not just the perfect opening chapter.
